name: Baking Powder Biscuits and Buttermilk Biscuits
recipeInstructions:
Baking Powder Biscuits:
1. In a medium mixing bowl, stir together flour, baking powder, and salt.
2. Using a pastry blender, cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. Make a well in the center of the mixture.
4. Add milk all at once and stir just with a fork until moistened.
5. Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface.
6. Quickly knead dough by gently folding and pressing dough 10 to 12 times or until nearly smooth.
7. Pat or roll dough to 1/2-inch thickness.
8. Cut dough with a 2-1/2-inch biscuit cutter (a glass works well if you don't have a cutter).
9. Place biscuits 1 inch apart on an ungreased baking sheet.
10. Bake in a 450°F oven for 10 to 12 minutes or until golden. Remove from the baking sheet and serve warm. Makes 10 biscuits.
Drop Biscuits:
1. Prepare dough as directed above, but increase milk to 1 cup.
2. Do not knead, roll, or cut the dough.
3. Using a large spoon, drop dough into 12 mounds on a greased baking sheet.
4. Bake as directed above. Makes 12 biscuits.
Buttermilk Biscuits:
1. Prepare dough as directed above, but stir in 1/4 teaspoon baking soda and substitute buttermilk or sour milk for the milk.
2. Bake as directed above. Makes 10 to 12 biscuits.
